title: Client Onboarding Automation for Real Estate
metaDescription: Done-for-you client onboarding for real estate brokers and property managers. New clients get welcomed, set up, and moving inside one business day.
excerpt: A done-for-you onboarding sequence that turns a signed real estate client into a fully set up account inside one business day, without the owner chasing forms.

sections.intro:
In real estate, the moment a buyer, seller, or landlord signs is the moment things tend to go quiet. You meant to send the welcome email, the disclosures, the photographer brief, and the calendar invite for the kickoff call, but three more leads came in and a closing slipped. By the time the new client hears from you again, they are wondering if they picked the right agent or property manager.

Client Onboarding Automation is the Capability inside your AI Operating System that handles that first 48 hours for you. The moment a contract is signed or a management agreement is countersigned, the system kicks off the welcome sequence, collects what you need, books the kickoff, and writes the file into your CRM, all in your voice. You stay the agent. The system handles the steps you already know by heart.
sections.fourCFit:
This page covers a Capability inside the AIOS. Capabilities are the done-for-you workflows that actually do the work. Onboarding sits next to the other three layers:

  • Context: your voice, your offers, your buyer vs. seller vs. landlord checklists, your service area, and your compliance language.
  • Connections: your CRM, e-sign tool, inbox, calendar, document storage, and listing or property management software.
  • Capabilities: this page, plus lead response, listing follow-up, tenant communication, and closing reminders.
  • Cadence: the onboarding sequence runs the second a deal moves to signed, and nudges the client and your team until every step is complete.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
When this capability is installed, here is the artifact you get and the service level it runs at:

  • A new client record created in your CRM within 2 minutes of contract execution, tagged by deal type (buyer, seller, landlord, tenant).
  • A welcome email and SMS sent in your voice within 5 minutes, with what happens next, who their point of contact is, and the link to book the kickoff call.
  • A short intake form sent automatically, asking only for what you do not already have (preferred contact times, lender or insurance contact, property access details, pets, parking, photography access windows).
  • Disclosures, agency forms, and W-9 or owner packets routed for signature on the right template per deal type.
  • Kickoff call booked on your calendar within 24 hours, with a pre-call brief written for you the morning of.
  • A shared client folder created, named to your convention, with the executed contract filed inside.
  • Internal Slack or email handoff to your transaction coordinator, photographer, or property manager with the relevant details.
  • A daily exception report listing any client who has not completed onboarding within 48 hours so nobody falls through.
sections.howWeInstall:
Installing this capability follows the standard AIOS rollout. No long discovery, no consulting deck.

  1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes). We look at your current onboarding, how many deals per month, where things stall, and which tools you already pay for. Run the audit to get a written plan.
  2. Install (10 business days). We build the onboarding capability against your real CRM, e-sign, calendar, and storage. We write the emails and SMS in your voice, set the rules for buyer vs. seller vs. landlord, and dry-run it on a test deal before it touches a real client.
  3. Operate (ongoing). The system runs every time a contract closes. We add one new automation per week, watch the exception report, and tune the language as your market shifts. See plans for what Operate includes.
sections.expectedResults:
Grounded ranges from real estate teams running this capability:

  • Time from signed contract to client fully set up drops from 2 to 5 days down to under 24 hours.
  • Agent or broker admin time per new client drops by 60 to 120 minutes.
  • Intake form completion rises from roughly 50 percent to 85 percent or higher, because the ask is smaller and timed right.
  • Kickoff call no-shows drop, because the booking and reminders are automatic.
  • Compliance gaps (missing disclosures, unsigned agency forms) drop close to zero, because the exception report catches them on day two.

Standing guarantee: across your full AIOS, you recover 40 or more hours a month or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
Will the welcome messages sound like an AI wrote them?

No. We write them once, in your voice, using your actual phrases. The system sends them, it does not invent them. If you want a sentence changed, we change it and every future client gets the new version.

Does this work for both sales and property management?

Yes. The capability branches by deal type. A buyer gets a lender intro and showing logistics. A seller gets the photographer brief and pricing prep. A new landlord gets the owner packet, W-9, and management agreement. A new tenant gets move-in instructions and the resident portal invite.

What if a client does not respond to the intake form?

The cadence layer follows up at 24 and 72 hours, then escalates to a one-line note from you. The daily exception report flags anyone still incomplete at 48 hours so your coordinator can call them directly. Nothing sits silently.
